text: Three representative training intensity distribution models exist for endurance sports: pyramidal (PYR), threshold (THR), and polarized (POL), each with distinct structural characteristics and application scenarios.
Each training intensity distribution model can significantly improve performance when aligned with the specific demands of training phases and sport-specific tasks.
Training intensity distribution undergoes dynamic structural shifts across periodization phases: general preparation, specific preparation, pre-competition, and competition.
The choice of training intensity distribution model should be determined by sport characteristics, training objectives, and individual variability rather than applying a single model universally.

Strategic integration of multiple training intensity distribution models across training phases may enhance cumulative training adaptations and competitive performance compared to using a single model throughout.

Pyramidal training intensity distribution features the highest volume in low-intensity zones with progressively decreasing volume at higher intensities, while polarized distribution emphasizes high volumes of low-intensity and high-intensity work with minimal threshold training.
